  "text": "The Kubernetes memory-pressure-based pod eviction logic is spread across several files in pkg/kubelet/eviction/:\n\n1. **Threshold Definitions** (`pkg/kubelet/eviction/defaults_linux.go`, `defaults_others.go`, `defaults_windows.go`): Define `DefaultEvictionHard`, which sets the default hard eviction threshold for `memory.available` (100Mi on Linux/others, 500Mi on Windows). These are the defaults applied when no custom thresholds are configured.\n\n2. **Eviction API Types** (`pkg/kubelet/eviction/api/types.go`): Defines `SignalMemoryAvailable` (the signal `\"memory.available\"`), `Threshold`, `ThresholdOperator`, and `OpForSignal` map — the core data types for expressing memory eviction thresholds.\n\n3. **Eviction Config Types** (`pkg/kubelet/eviction/types.go`): Defines `Config` (holds `Thresholds []evictionapi.Threshold`, `PressureTransitionPeriod`, `KernelMemcgNotification`) and the `ThresholdNotifier` interface for kernel memcg-based notifications.\n\n4. **Eviction Manager** (`pkg/kubelet/eviction/eviction_manager.go`): The main decision logic. `synchronize()` is the control loop that evaluates thresholds against current node stats, determines if memory pressure is active, selects pods for eviction using `rankMemoryPressure`, and kills pods. `IsUnderMemoryPressure()` exposes the current memory pressure state. `Start()` launches the control loop and optionally hooks into kernel memcg notifications via `NewMemoryThresholdNotifier`.\n\n5. **Eviction Helpers** (`pkg/kubelet/eviction/helpers.go`): Contains `rankMemoryPressure()` (orders pods for eviction by whether usage exceeds requests, priority, and memory usage), `addAllocatableThresholds()` (copies `SignalMemoryAvailable` hard thresholds to `SignalAllocatableMemoryAvailable`), and `isHardEvictionThreshold()`.\n\n6. **Memory Threshold Notifiers** (`pkg/kubelet/eviction/memory_threshold_notifier_others.go`, `memory_threshold_notifier_windows.go`, `threshold_notifier_linux.go`): Implement kernel memcg-based notifications so the eviction manager can react immediately when memory thresholds are crossed, rather than only on polling intervals."
}

### Files defining memory pressure eviction thresholds:

|------|---------|
| `pkg/kubelet/eviction/defaults_linux.go` | `DefaultEvictionHard` — default `memory.available < 100Mi` threshold for Linux |

| `pkg/kubelet/eviction/memory_threshold_notifier_windows.go` | `NewMemoryThresholdNotifier` for Windows |
| `pkg/kubelet/eviction/threshold_notifier_linux.go` | Cgroup notifier using kernel memcg events |
